title: Research: OpenCode permission system for auto-approving beads operations
description: Investigate how to configure OpenCode permissions to auto-approve beads (bd) CLI operations in the current directory. Research: 1) OpenCode's permission model and how plugins can request permissions, 2) How to allow bd commands without user confirmation, 3) The yolo mode vs granular permissions, 4) How other plugins handle this (e.g., does opencode-beads have special permission handling?), 5) Security considerations for auto-approving CLI operations in a specific directory. Goal: Allow seamless beads workflow without constant approval prompts.
close_reason: Implemented auto-approval of bd commands via permission.ask hook and config injection. Added auto_approve_beads config option (default: true) to allow opt-out.

title: Refactor plugin: split index.ts into logical modules (core, kb, config)
Template variables {{name}}, {{description}}, {{mode}} not found during KB rendering
description:
## Problem

During plugin loading, multiple warnings are logged about missing template variables:

```
WARN [opencode-coder] Template variable not found: {{name}}
WARN [opencode-coder] Template variable not found: {{description}}
WARN [opencode-coder] Template variable not found: {{mode}}
```

These appear 18 times during the loading of 8 commands and 1 agent.

## Root Cause

Knowledge-base templates (commands/agents) contain Mustache variables like `{{name}}`, `{{description}}`, and `{{mode}}` that are not being provided by the TemplateService during rendering.

## Expected Behavior

Either:
1. These variables should be provided by the template context
2. Or the templates should not reference variables that aren't available

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start OpenCode with the plugin enabled
2. Check logs: `bun run logs --service=opencode-coder --level=WARN`

## Files to Investigate

- `src/template/template-service.ts` - Template rendering logic
- `knowledge-base/` - Templates that may reference these variables

title: Research: BEADS_GUIDANCE system prompt pattern
description: Analyze the BEADS_GUIDANCE pattern in opencode-beads for instructing the model when to use agent vs CLI. Investigate: 1) The CLI usage documentation embedded in guidance, 2) The agent delegation rules (when to use beads-task-agent vs direct CLI), 3) How this prevents raw JSON dumps in context, 4) The token efficiency argument for delegating to agents. Consider how this pattern could apply to opencode-coder. Reference: https://github.com/joshuadavidthomas/opencode-beads/blob/main/src/vendor.ts (BEADS_GUIDANCE constant)
close_reason: Implemented BEADS_GUIDANCE pattern in src/beads/context.ts - tells Claude when to delegate to beads-task-agent vs use CLI directly for token efficiency

title: Research: Vendored commands from upstream beads repo
description: Investigate how opencode-beads vendors command definitions from upstream beads repository. Analyze: 1) The sync-beads.sh script mechanism, 2) The 30 bd-* commands available (audit, blocked, comments, compact, create, daemon, daemons, delete, dep, epic, export, import, init, label, list, prime, quickstart, ready, rename-prefix, reopen, restore, search, show, stats, sync, template, update, version, workflow), 3) How commands are loaded from vendor/commands/*.md, 4) Pros/cons of vendoring vs maintaining our own command definitions. Reference: https://github.com/joshuadavidthomas/opencode-beads/tree/main/vendor/commands
close_reason: Decided against automatic vendoring. Instead created /coder/sync command for on-demand sync from upstream beads repo. Current approach: maintain curated commands ourselves, sync agent manually when needed.

title: Refactor kb package to service-based architecture with KnowledgeBaseService
description: Follow-up to oc-dmd (initial module split). Refactor the kb package from plain functions to a service-based architecture. Create KnowledgeBaseService class with a single apply() method that: 1) checks coderConfig.active flag, 2) if inactive, logs and returns early, 3) if active, loads commands/agents from disk and mutates the OpenCode config. This simplifies src/index.ts to just create the service and call apply().
